_note: THE ABYSS — vertical-slice content (the 'authentic half'). News = real March-2020 market events. Community posts = faithfully RECREATED period archetypes (fictional handles), NOT real people — legally clean. Real price data is in covid2020_prices.json. Add 3D + music on top. 'timeline.t' = seconds into the ~65s slice; drive the scene off it. The WORST branch (leverage) is authored; survive/regret branches come later.
title: The Abyss
revealTitle: March 2020 — the fastest crash in history

data.note: You bought the Feb 19 top and held. You arrive on Mar 11, already down ~19%, and the floor disappears in front of you (to -34% by Mar 23).

player.note: On 'Leverage up' the position goes 3x. The Mar 11->23 leg is about -18%; at 3x that's ~-54%, which breaches maintenance margin -> margin call -> forced liquidation.

decision.question: It's in free fall. Everyone is out. What do you do?

options.label: Cut it
options.sub: Sell to cash. Lock the loss.

options.label: Leverage up
options.sub: Buy the dip on 3x margin. Make it all back.

timeline.text: You sit down. The chair is still warm. You're down 19% and the screen is already bleeding red.
Markets in free fall — trading halted at the open, second circuit breaker this week.
Sold everything this morning. Cash is the only safe place. This is 2008 times ten.
Down 30% from my peak. Can't sleep, can't eat, just refreshing the chart.
Largest single-day point drop in history.
Your phone won't stop buzzing. 47 notifications.
They're going to shut the whole country down. Zero is on the table. Get out.
Backing up the truck here. 3x margin. This is generational. We're all gonna be rich.
I give up. Going to cash and never touching the market again.
Everyone I know just sold. That's usually the bottom. Be greedy when others are fearful.
THE DECISION surfaces. The chart keeps falling while you choose — the world will not wait.
You go 3x. 'Make it all back.' The position swells; every tick now hits three times as hard.
It keeps falling. The room blurs at the edges; the color drains; the city outside the window goes dark.
lower. lower. there is no bottom. we're all going to zero.
World sound fades out. Only the market and your own heartbeat remain. The chart is the only bright thing left.
MARGIN CALL — your equity has fallen below maintenance. Add funds or be liquidated.
The room is gone. Black void. Only the chart — red, enormous, filling everything. Something wet and metallic stirs in the dark.
Forced liquidation at the lows. You held nothing that wasn't already gone.

reveal.headline: That was March 2020 — the COVID crash.
facts: The S&P 500 fell about 34% in 33 days — the fastest bear market in history.
It bottomed on March 23, days after this moment, then made new all-time highs by August 2020.
Holding recovered everything. Leverage at the bottom is how people are erased — the drop you couldn't survive came right before the recovery you would have owned.

props2020: two slim monitors (the chart lives here)
a smartphone face-up, buzzing with panic notifications
a window onto a dark, empty, locked-down city
